prompt_text: Compare Conveyor and SafeBase for automate security questionnaire responses so sales deals do not get stuck in security review
response:
If your main goal is to **automate security questionnaire responses** so deals don’t stall in security review, **Conveyor** is the stronger fit based on the available information. SafeBase appears to be more of a **trust center** platform, while Conveyor explicitly positions its AI as covering both **security questionnaire automation** and trust center workflows.[2][5]

| Area | Conveyor | SafeBase |
|---|---|---|
| Core fit for security questionnaires | Built specifically to generate, review, and export questionnaire answers from docs, Q&A pairs, past answers, and external sources.[2][3] | Described by Conveyor as a *trust center solution*; the provided results do not show equally detailed questionnaire-automation capabilities for SafeBase.[5] |
| Speed / automation | Claims 95%+ first-pass accuracy and about 91% reduction in time spent on questionnaires.[2] Conveyor also says teams can respond 90% faster.[3] | No comparable automation-performance claims appear in the provided results.[5] |
| Input sources | Uses documents, Q&A pairs, past answers, shared drives, wikis, company websites, and support docs.[1][2][3] | Not detailed in the provided results. |
| Questionnaire formats | Handles Word, PDF, Excel, and portal-based questionnaires, including browser-extension autocomplete.[1][2][3] | Not detailed in the provided results. |
| Sales workflow impact | Explicitly designed to keep presales and sales teams moving and tie security work to revenue.[2] | Not shown in the provided results. |

Conveyor’s materials emphasize that it can import spreadsheets, Word docs, PDFs, and portal links, extract questions, draft cited answers, let reviewers edit/confidence-check them, and then export back into the original format or autocomplete portal forms.[1][2][3] It also says this reduces questionnaire time dramatically and helps keep sales teams in their workflow.[2][3]

For SafeBase, the provided evidence only clearly supports that it is a **trust center** product and that Conveyor claims to pair questionnaire automation with trust-center capabilities as well.[5] Based on the search results you provided, there is not enough detail to show SafeBase is as strong as Conveyor for automated questionnaire response generation specifically.[5]

If you want, I can also turn this into a **buying scorecard** for sales, security, and RevOps with the decision criteria that matter most.
